sound_type: descending whinny + keek -- musical descending whinny (horse-like) and sharp explosive 'keek!' contact call; E=4
ecosystem: Freshwater marsh -- cattails, bulrushes, sedges, shallow water. More tolerant of varied wetland types than Virginia Rail.
season: Summer breeder in western Washington. Migrant. Peak vocalization spring through mid-summer.

coordinates.description: Sora in freshwater marshes of Puget Sound lowlands. Cattails, bulrushes. Overlaps with Virginia Rail.

taxonomy_note.description: SECOND RALLIDAE. Completes the Virginia Rail/Sora comparison pair (161-162). Most common North American rail. Almost never seen. Short, stubby, chicken-like bill. Surface-picking feeder.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Rallidae: second entry. Virginia Rail (161) + Sora (162). American Coot (164) pending.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Porzana possibly from Italian dialect for a small bird. carolina for Carolina, where the species was first described.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Porzana -- the crakes. Short-billed, laterally compressed, marsh specialists. Worldwide genus.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Second Rallidae. Completes the Virginia Rail/Sora comparison pair.

song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: 2000-6000 Hz combined. Higher than Virginia Rail.
song_metrics.note_structure: WHINNY: rapid descending whistled notes 'whee-hee-hee-hee-hee-he-he-he.' Musical, horse-like. KEEK: sharp explosive single syllable 'keek!' or 'kee-EEK!'
song_metrics.note_types: Descending whinny (territorial/contact), sharp keek (contact/alarm -- most diagnostic), per-WEEP (breeding), soft clucking (close range).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Whinny as descending phrase. Keek as single explosive note. Both from concealed positions.
song_metrics.timbre: Whinny: musical, whistled, horse-like. Higher and more musical than Virginia Rail. Keek: sharp, piercing, knife-like.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Carries 100-250 meters. Keek carries well despite brevity due to high frequency and sharp attack.
song_metrics.tempo: Whinny accelerates through phrase. Keek at irregular intervals.
song_metrics.context: Territorial. Contact. Breeding. Year-round (keek). Dawn/dusk peak for whinny.
song_metrics.key_property: DESCENDING WHINNY + SHARP KEEK -- dual vocalization. SECOND RALLIDAE. Most common N.A. rail, almost never seen. Abundance paradox. E=4. v1.49.302.

plumage.overall: Gray-brown above, gray below. Black face patch. Black central breast patch. Barred flanks. Cryptic in marsh.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: Short, stubby, yellow bill with black base. Black face and breast patches. Small size.
plumage.flight_pattern: Weak, brief, fluttering flight with dangling legs. Last resort.

physical_description.overall: Length: 20-25 cm. Weight: ~75 g. Laterally compressed. Short-billed.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Short yellow bill. Black face patch. Laterally compressed.
physical_description.field_id: Almost never seen. Detected by whinny or keek from marsh. If seen: small, dark, laterally compressed bird with short yellow bill, briefly visible at cattail edge.

nodes.title: Descending Whinny
nodes.description: Musical descending series of whistled notes. 2000-5000 Hz. Horse-like. Higher-pitched and more musical than Virginia Rail. 8-15 notes in 1-2 seconds.

nodes.title: Sharp Keek
nodes.description: Explosive single-syllable 'keek!' 3000-6000 Hz. Cuts through marsh soundscape. Most diagnostic call. Virginia Rail lacks this. Knife-edge sharpness.

nodes.title: Abundance-Invisibility Paradox
nodes.description: Most common rail in N.A. (700K-1M+). Almost never seen. Voice-to-sight detection 20:1 or higher. The defining paradox of the species.

nodes.title: Short-Billed Rail
nodes.description: Short, stubby, chicken-like bill. Surface-picking adaptation (seeds, insects). Contrast with Virginia Rail's long probing bill. Bill shape drives niche partitioning.

nodes.title: Virginia Rail / Sora Comparison (161-162)
nodes.description: Long bill vs. short bill. Whinny grunt vs. whinny + keek. Probing vs. surface-picking. Same marsh, different niches. Both E=4. The Rallidae comparison pair.

cross_references.relationship: RALLIDAE COMPARISON PAIR! Virginia Rail (161) + Sora (162). Long bill vs. short bill. Same marsh, different niches. E=4 + E=4.
RALLIDAE RANGE! Sora: invisible in marsh. Coot: visible on open water. Both Rallidae. Full behavioral spectrum.
MARSH SOUNDSCAPE! Sora keek (high, sharp) + Bittern boom (low, resonant). Complementary frequencies. Three marsh voices in three degrees.
